subject: Computer Networking
title: SIP: Setting Up Calls
description: Learn SIP: Setting Up Calls in one minute.

hook.text: Before a voice call begins, both sides must agree on codecs, ports, and addresses. SIP negotiates all of that.

buildup.text: SIP — Session Initiation Protocol — handles call setup, modification, and teardown. It doesn't carry the audio.

discovery.text: SIP is text-based, like HTTP. An INVITE message starts a call. BYE ends it. Simple request-response pattern.

twist.text: SIP struggles with NAT. Special servers called STUN and TURN help punch through firewalls for direct connections.

climax.text: Most VoIP phone systems use SIP. It replaced the old telephone signaling systems with internet-native protocols.

punchline.text: SIP is the matchmaker. RTP is the conversation.

quiz.question: What does SIP do in a VoIP call?
options: Sets up, modifies, and tears down the call session
